papers.title: HY-World 2.0: A Multi-Modal World Model for Reconstructing, Generating, and Simulating 3D Worlds
papers.abstract: Abstract HY-World 2.0 is a multi-modal world model framework that generates high-fidelity 3D Gaussian Splatting scenes from diverse inputs using specialized modules for panorama generation, trajectory planning, world expansion, and composition, along with an enhanced rendering platform for interactive 3D exploration.  AI-generated summary We introduce HY-World 2.0, a multi-modal world model framework that advances our prior project HY-World 1.0. HY-World 2.0 accommodates diverse input modalities, including text prompts, single-view images, multi-view images, and videos, and produces 3D world representations. With text or single-view image inputs, the model performs world generation, synthesizing high-fidelity, navigable 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) scenes. This is achieved through a four-stage method: a) Panorama Generation with HY-Pano 2.0, b) Trajectory Planning with WorldNav, c) World Expansion with WorldStereo 2.0, and d) World Composition with WorldMirror 2.0. Specifically, we introduce key innovations to enhance panorama fidelity, enable 3D scene understanding and planning, and upgrade WorldStereo, our keyframe-based view generation model with consistent memory. We also upgrade WorldMirror, a feed-forward model for universal 3D prediction, by refining model architecture and learning strategy, enabling world reconstruction from multi-view images or videos. Also, we introduce WorldLens, a high-performance 3DGS rendering platform featuring a flexible engine-agnostic architecture, automatic IBL lighting, efficient collision detection, and training-rendering co-design, enabling interactive exploration of 3D worlds with character support. Extensive experiments demonstrate that HY-World 2.0 achieves state-of-the-art performance on several benchmarks among open-source approaches, delivering results comparable to the closed-source model Marble. We release all model weights, code, and technical details to facilitate reproducibility and support further research on 3D world models.

ru.title: От изображения к интерактивному 3D миру: универсальная генерация виртуальных сцен
ru.desc: HY-World 2.0 — это многомодальная модель мира, которая генерирует высокофиделитные 3D сцены на основе различных входных данных: текстовых описаний, одного или нескольких изображений, видео. Система использует специализированные модули для создания панорам, планирования траекторий движения, расширения сцены и композиции элементов, применяя технологию 3D Gaussian Splatting. Авторы также представляют WorldLens — платформу для эффективного рендеринга и интерактивного исследования сгенерированных 3D миров с поддержкой персонажей и автоматического освещения. Фреймворк достигает лучших результатов среди открытых решений и конкурирует с закрытыми коммерческими моделями.

en.title: Revolutionizing 3D World Generation with HY-World 2.0
en.desc: HY-World 2.0 is an advanced multi-modal world model framework that generates detailed 3D scenes from various input types, such as text and images. It utilizes a four-stage process that includes panorama generation, trajectory planning, world expansion, and composition to create high-fidelity 3D Gaussian Splatting scenes. The framework introduces significant improvements in panorama quality and scene understanding, along with an upgraded rendering platform for interactive exploration. Extensive testing shows that HY-World 2.0 outperforms many open-source models, providing results that rival those of proprietary systems.

zh.title: HY-World 2.0：多模态3D世界生成的未来
zh.desc: HY-World 2.0 是一个多模态世界模型框架，能够从多种输入生成高保真度的3D高斯点云场景。该模型支持文本提示、单视图图像、多视图图像和视频等多种输入方式，并通过四个阶段的方法实现世界生成。具体来说，HY-World 2.0 引入了全景生成、轨迹规划、世界扩展和世界组合等模块，提升了全景的保真度和3D场景理解能力。通过大量实验，HY-World 2.0 在多个基准测试中表现出色，达到了与闭源模型相当的效果。

papers.title: RAD-2: Scaling Reinforcement Learning in a Generator-Discriminator Framework
papers.abstract: Abstract A unified generator-discriminator framework for autonomous driving motion planning that improves stability and performance through diffusion-based trajectory generation and reinforcement learning optimization.  AI-generated summary High-level autonomous driving requires motion planners capable of modeling multimodal future uncertainties while remaining robust in closed-loop interactions. Although diffusion-based planners are effective at modeling complex trajectory distributions, they often suffer from stochastic instabilities and the lack of corrective negative feedback when trained purely with imitation learning. To address these issues, we propose RAD-2, a unified generator-discriminator framework for closed-loop planning. Specifically, a diffusion-based generator is used to produce diverse trajectory candidates, while an RL-optimized discriminator reranks these candidates according to their long-term driving quality. This decoupled design avoids directly applying sparse scalar rewards to the full high-dimensional trajectory space, thereby improving optimization stability. To further enhance reinforcement learning, we introduce Temporally Consistent Group Relative Policy Optimization, which exploits temporal coherence to alleviate the credit assignment problem. In addition, we propose On-policy Generator Optimization, which converts closed-loop feedback into structured longitudinal optimization signals and progressively shifts the generator toward high-reward trajectory manifolds. To support efficient large-scale training, we introduce BEV-Warp, a high-throughput simulation environment that performs closed-loop evaluation directly in Bird's-Eye View feature space via spatial warping. RAD-2 reduces the collision rate by 56% compared with strong diffusion-based planners. Real-world deployment further demonstrates improved perceived safety and driving smoothness in complex urban traffic.

ru.title: Диффузионное планирование с дискриминативной обратной связью для безопасного автономного вождения
ru.desc: В статье предлагается RAD-2 — единая архитектура генератор-дискриминатор для планирования траекторий в автономном вождении. Диффузионная модель генерирует разнообразные кандидаты траекторий, а оптимизированный методом reinforcement learning дискриминатор переранжирует их по качеству вождения. Авторы вводят Temporally Consistent Group Relative Policy Optimization для решения проблемы распределения кредита и On-policy Generator Optimization для смещения генератора к высокознаграждаемым многообразиям траекторий. Предложенный подход снижает количество столкновений на 56% и демонстрирует улучшение безопасности и плавности вождения при реальном развертывании в городском трафике.

en.title: RAD-2: Smarter Motion Planning for Safer Autonomous Driving
en.desc: This paper presents RAD-2, a novel framework for motion planning in autonomous driving that combines a generator and a discriminator to enhance trajectory generation. The generator uses diffusion techniques to create a variety of potential driving paths, while the discriminator, optimized through reinforcement learning, evaluates and ranks these paths based on their effectiveness in real-world driving scenarios. By decoupling the generation and evaluation processes, the framework improves stability and reduces the impact of sparse rewards on trajectory optimization. Additionally, the paper introduces innovative techniques for reinforcement learning that enhance the training process, leading to a significant reduction in collision rates and improved driving performance in complex environments.

zh.title: 提升自主驾驶的稳定性与性能
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种统一的生成器-判别器框架RAD-2，用于自主驾驶的运动规划。该框架通过扩散式轨迹生成和强化学习优化，提高了稳定性和性能。生成器生成多样化的轨迹候选，而判别器则根据长期驾驶质量对这些候选进行重新排序。通过引入时间一致的相对策略优化和在线生成器优化，进一步增强了强化学习的效果，最终在复杂城市交通中显著降低了碰撞率。

papers.title: HiVLA: A Visual-Grounded-Centric Hierarchical Embodied Manipulation System
papers.abstract: Abstract HiVLA presents a hierarchical vision-language-action framework that decouples semantic planning from motor control using a diffusion transformer action expert with cascaded cross-attention for improved robotic manipulation.  AI-generated summary While end-to-end Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models offer a promising paradigm for robotic manipulation, fine-tuning them on narrow control data often compromises the profound reasoning capabilities inherited from their base Vision-Language Models (VLMs). To resolve this fundamental trade-off, we propose HiVLA, a visual-grounded-centric hierarchical framework that explicitly decouples high-level semantic planning from low-level motor control. In high-level part, a VLM planner first performs task decomposition and visual grounding to generate structured plans, comprising a subtask instruction and a precise target bounding box. Then, to translate this plan into physical actions, we introduce a flow-matching Diffusion Transformer (DiT) action expert in low-level part equipped with a novel cascaded cross-attention mechanism. This design sequentially fuses global context, high-resolution object-centric crops and skill semantics, enabling the DiT to focus purely on robust execution. Our decoupled architecture preserves the VLM's zero-shot reasoning while allowing independent improvement of both components. Extensive experiments in simulation and the real world demonstrate that HiVLA significantly outperforms state-of-the-art end-to-end baselines, particularly excelling in long-horizon skill composition and the fine-grained manipulation of small objects in cluttered scenes.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Разделение ума и рук: иерархия для робота
ru.desc: HiVLA представляет иерархический фреймворк для робототехники, который разделяет семантическое планирование и моторное управление. В верхнем уровне модель зрения и языка выполняет декомпозицию задач и визуальное закрепление, генерируя структурированные планы. На нижнем уровне трансформер диффузии с каскадным кросс-вниманием переводит эти планы в физические действия робота. Такой подход сохраняет способности языковой модели к рассуждениям, одновременно позволяя независимо улучшать оба компонента архитектуры.

en.title: Decoupling Planning and Control for Smarter Robotics
en.desc: HiVLA is a new framework that separates high-level planning from low-level motor control in robotic manipulation. It uses a Vision-Language Model (VLM) to create structured plans that include specific tasks and target locations. For executing these plans, it employs a Diffusion Transformer (DiT) that focuses on detailed actions while considering the overall context. This separation allows for better reasoning and performance in complex tasks, especially when manipulating small objects in cluttered environments.

zh.title: 解耦语义规划与运动控制的机器人框架
zh.desc: HiVLA是一个层次化的视觉-语言-动作框架，它将语义规划与运动控制解耦。该框架使用扩散变换器动作专家和级联交叉注意力机制，提升了机器人操作的效果。在高层次部分，视觉语言模型（VLM）规划器进行任务分解和视觉定位，生成结构化的计划。在低层次部分，流匹配扩散变换器专注于将计划转化为物理动作，从而实现更稳健的执行。

papers.title: ASGuard: Activation-Scaling Guard to Mitigate Targeted Jailbreaking Attack
papers.abstract: Abstract Activation-Scaling Guard (ASGuard) mitigates brittle refusal behaviors in large language models by identifying and recalibrating specific attention heads vulnerable to tense-based jailbreaking attacks through mechanistic circuit analysis and targeted fine-tuning.  AI-generated summary Large language models (LLMs), despite being safety-aligned, exhibit brittle refusal behaviors that can be circumvented by simple linguistic changes. As tense jailbreaking demonstrates that models refusing harmful requests often comply when rephrased in past tense, a critical generalization gap is revealed in current alignment methods whose underlying mechanisms are poorly understood. In this work, we introduce Activation-Scaling Guard (ASGuard), an insightful, mechanistically-informed framework that surgically mitigates this specific vulnerability. In the first step, we use circuit analysis to identify the specific attention heads causally linked to the targeted jailbreaking such as a tense-changing attack. Second, we train a precise, channel-wise scaling vector to recalibrate the activation of tense vulnerable heads. Lastly, we apply it into a "preventative fine-tuning", forcing the model to learn a more robust refusal mechanism. Across four LLMs, ASGuard effectively reduces the attack success rate of targeted jailbreaking while preserving general capabilities and minimizing over refusal, achieving a Pareto-optimal balance between safety and utility. Our findings underscore how adversarial suffixes suppress the propagation of the refusal-mediating direction, based on mechanistic analysis. Furthermore, our work showcases how a deep understanding of model internals can be leveraged to develop practical, efficient, and targeted methods for adjusting model behavior, charting a course for more reliable and interpretable AI safety.

ru.title: Механистическая защита: укрепление отказов LLM через анализ внимания
ru.desc: В работе представлен метод Activation-Scaling Guard (ASGuard), который повышает устойчивость больших языковых моделей к атакам типа jailbreaking путём изменения грамматического времени. Авторы используют механистический анализ схем внимания для выявления конкретных attention heads, уязвимых к таким атакам. Затем они обучают вектор масштабирования на уровне каналов для калибровки активаций уязвимых голов и применяют профилактический fine-tuning. ASGuard эффективно снижает успешность целевых атак на четырёх LLM моделях, сохраняя общие способности и минимизируя чрезмерный отказ в выполнении безопасных запросов.

en.title: Strengthening AI Safety with Targeted Attention Calibration
en.desc: The paper introduces Activation-Scaling Guard (ASGuard), a method designed to improve the safety of large language models (LLMs) by addressing their vulnerabilities to jailbreaking attacks, particularly those that exploit tense changes in language. By using mechanistic circuit analysis, ASGuard identifies specific attention heads that are susceptible to these attacks and recalibrates their activations through targeted fine-tuning. This approach not only reduces the success rate of such attacks but also maintains the model's overall performance and minimizes unnecessary refusals. The study highlights the importance of understanding model internals to create effective strategies for enhancing AI safety and reliability.

zh.title: 提升AI安全性的激活缩放保护
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种名为激活缩放保护（ASGuard）的方法，旨在减轻大型语言模型在面对特定攻击时的脆弱拒绝行为。通过机制电路分析，ASGuard能够识别出与时态变化攻击相关的特定注意力头，并对其进行重新校准。该方法通过精确的通道缩放向量进行训练，增强模型的拒绝机制，从而提高其安全性。实验结果表明，ASGuard在四种大型语言模型中有效降低了攻击成功率，同时保持了模型的整体能力。

papers.title: How to Fine-Tune a Reasoning Model? A Teacher-Student Cooperation Framework to Synthesize Student-Consistent SFT Data
papers.abstract: Abstract Teacher-student cooperation data synthesis framework addresses stylistic divergence in synthetic data for improved model fine-tuning performance.  AI-generated summary A widely adopted strategy for model enhancement is to use synthetic data generated by a stronger model for supervised fine-tuning (SFT). However, for emerging reasoning models like Qwen3-8B, this approach often fails to improve reasoning capabilities and can even lead to a substantial drop in performance. In this work, we identify substantial stylistic divergence between teacher generated data and the distribution of student as a major factor impacting SFT. To bridge this gap, we propose a Teacher-Student Cooperation Data Synthesis framework (TESSY), which interleaves teacher and student models to alternately generate style and non-style tokens. Consequently, TESSY produces synthetic sequences that inherit the advanced reasoning capabilities of the teacher while maintaining stylistic consistency with the distribution of the student. In experiments on code generation using GPT-OSS-120B as the teacher, fine-tuning Qwen3-8B on teacher-generated data leads to performance drops of 3.25% on LiveCodeBench-Pro and 10.02% on OJBench, whereas TESSY achieves improvements of 11.25% and 6.68%.

ru.title: Синтез данных через сотрудничество учителя и студента
ru.desc: В статье предложен метод TESSY для синтеза обучающих данных, который решает проблему стилистического несоответствия между данными, генерируемыми учительской моделью, и распределением данных студенческой модели. Авторы показывают, что прямое использование синтетических данных от более мощной модели часто приводит к снижению производительности при fine-tuning студенческой модели на задачах рассуждений. Предложенный подход чередует работу учительской и студенческой моделей при генерации токенов, позволяя сохранить продвинутые возможности рассуждения учителя при соответствии стилю студента. Экспериментальные результаты на задачах генерации кода демонстрируют значительное улучшение производительности вместо типичного падения при стандартном подходе.

en.title: Bridging the Gap: Enhancing Model Performance with TESSY
en.desc: This paper introduces a framework called TESSY, which stands for Teacher-Student Cooperation Data Synthesis, aimed at improving the performance of reasoning models during fine-tuning. The authors highlight that traditional methods using synthetic data from a stronger model often result in performance drops due to stylistic differences between the teacher and student models. TESSY addresses this issue by alternating the generation of style and non-style tokens, ensuring that the synthetic data retains the reasoning strengths of the teacher while aligning stylistically with the student. Experimental results show that TESSY significantly enhances the performance of the Qwen3-8B model compared to conventional methods, demonstrating its effectiveness in bridging the stylistic gap.

zh.title: 教师-学生合作，提升模型微调性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种教师-学生合作数据合成框架（TESSY），旨在解决合成数据中的风格差异问题，以提高模型微调性能。研究发现，教师生成的数据与学生模型的分布之间存在显著的风格差异，这对监督微调（SFT）产生了负面影响。TESSY通过交替生成风格和非风格标记，结合教师和学生模型的优势，生成具有一致风格的合成序列。实验结果表明，使用TESSY框架进行微调可以显著提升模型在代码生成任务上的表现。

papers.title: UniDoc-RL: Coarse-to-Fine Visual RAG with Hierarchical Actions and Dense Rewards
papers.abstract: Abstract UniDoc-RL introduces a reinforcement learning framework for LVLMs that jointly optimizes retrieval, reranking, visual perception, and reasoning through hierarchical decision-making and dense multi-reward supervision.  AI-generated summary Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) extends Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) with external visual knowledge. However, existing visual RAG systems typically rely on generic retrieval signals that overlook the fine-grained visual semantics essential for complex reasoning. To address this limitation, we propose UniDoc-RL, a unified reinforcement learning framework in which an LVLM agent jointly performs retrieval, reranking, active visual perception, and reasoning. UniDoc-RL formulates visual information acquisition as a sequential decision-making problem with a hierarchical action space. Specifically, it progressively refines visual evidence from coarse-grained document retrieval to fine-grained image selection and active region cropping, allowing the model to suppress irrelevant content and attend to information-dense regions. For effective end-to-end training, we introduce a dense multi-reward scheme that provides task-aware supervision for each action. Based on Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), UniDoc-RL aligns agent behavior with multiple objectives without relying on a separate value network. To support this training paradigm, we curate a comprehensive dataset of high-quality reasoning trajectories with fine-grained action annotations. Experiments on three benchmarks demonstrate that UniDoc-RL consistently surpasses state-of-the-art baselines, yielding up to 17.7% gains over prior RL-based methods.

ru.title: Иерархическое обучение с подкреплением для умного визуального поиска и рассуждения
ru.desc: UniDoc-RL представляет собой фреймворк обучения с подкреплением для больших визуально-языковых моделей, который совместно оптимизирует процессы поиска документов, переранжирования результатов, активного визуального восприятия и логического рассуждения. Система формулирует задачу получения визуальной информации как последовательное принятие решений с иерархическим пространством действий, позволяя модели прогрессивно уточнять доказательства от грубого поиска документов до точного выбора изображений и кропирования информативных регионов. Для эффективного обучения используется схема многоцелевого вознаграждения, обеспечивающая специализированный сигнал обратной связи для каждого действия, и метод Group Relative Policy Optimization без необходимости отдельной сети ценности. Экспериментальные результаты на трёх бенчмарках показывают превосходство метода над существующими подходами с прирост производительности до 17,7% в сравнении с предыдущими методами на основе обучения с подкреплением.

en.title: Reinforcing Visual Reasoning in LVLMs with UniDoc-RL
en.desc: UniDoc-RL is a novel reinforcement learning framework designed for Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) that enhances their ability to retrieve, rerank, perceive visual information, and reason effectively. It addresses the shortcomings of existing visual retrieval systems by incorporating a hierarchical decision-making process that allows for detailed visual evidence refinement. The framework employs a dense multi-reward supervision strategy to provide targeted feedback for each action taken by the model, facilitating better learning outcomes. Experimental results show that UniDoc-RL significantly outperforms previous methods, achieving notable improvements in reasoning tasks.

zh.title: UniDoc-RL：优化视觉推理的新框架
zh.desc: UniDoc-RL 是一个强化学习框架，专为大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）设计，旨在通过分层决策和密集多重奖励监督来优化检索、重排序、视觉感知和推理。该框架将视觉信息获取视为一个顺序决策问题，逐步从粗粒度文档检索精炼到细粒度图像选择和主动区域裁剪。通过引入密集多重奖励机制，UniDoc-RL 为每个动作提供任务感知的监督，从而实现有效的端到端训练。实验结果表明，UniDoc-RL 在多个基准测试中表现优异，超越了现有的最先进方法。

papers.title: LongAct: Harnessing Intrinsic Activation Patterns for Long-Context Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract LongAct improves long-context reasoning in LLMs by implementing saliency-guided sparse updates based on high-magnitude activation patterns in query and key vectors.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement Learning (RL) has emerged as a critical driver for enhancing the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). While recent advancements have focused on reward engineering or data synthesis, few studies exploit the model's intrinsic representation characteristics to guide the training process. In this paper, we first observe the presence of high-magnitude activations within the query and key vectors when processing long contexts. Drawing inspiration from model quantization -- which establishes the criticality of such high-magnitude activations -- and the insight that long-context reasoning inherently exhibits a sparse structure, we hypothesize that these weights serve as the pivotal drivers for effective model optimization. Based on this insight, we propose LongAct, a strategy that shifts from uniform to saliency-guided sparse updates. By selectively updating only the weights associated with these significant activations, LongAct achieves an approximate 8% improvement on LongBench v2 and enhances generalization on the RULER benchmark. Furthermore, our method exhibits remarkable universality, consistently boosting performance across diverse RL algorithms such as GRPO and DAPO. Extensive ablation studies suggest that focusing on these salient features is key to unlocking long-context potential.
affiliations: Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications

ru.title: Точечные обновления вместо полной настройки: разреженная оптимизация для длинных контекстов
ru.desc: Статья представляет LongAct — метод для улучшения способности больших языковых моделей работать с длинными контекстами через обучение с подкреплением. Авторы обнаружили, что при обработке протяжённых текстов в векторах запросов и ключей появляются активации с большой амплитудой, которые являются критически важными для оптимизации модели. Предложенный подход использует разреженные обновления параметров, сосредотачиваясь только на весах, связанных с этими значимыми активациями, вместо равномерного обновления всех параметров. Метод достигает 8% улучшения на бенчмарке LongBench v2 и универсален для различных алгоритмов обучения с подкреплением.

en.title: Enhancing Long-Context Reasoning with Saliency-Guided Updates
en.desc: The paper introduces LongAct, a method designed to enhance long-context reasoning in Large Language Models (LLMs) by utilizing saliency-guided sparse updates. It identifies high-magnitude activations in query and key vectors as crucial for optimizing model performance. By selectively updating only the weights linked to these significant activations, LongAct improves reasoning capabilities and generalization across various reinforcement learning algorithms. The results show an approximate 8% performance boost on the LongBench v2 benchmark, highlighting the importance of focusing on salient features for effective model training.

zh.title: 显著性引导的稀疏更新提升长上下文推理
zh.desc: LongAct通过基于查询和键向量中的高幅度激活模式，实施显著性引导的稀疏更新，从而改善大型语言模型（LLMs）在长上下文推理中的表现。研究表明，在处理长上下文时，查询和键向量中存在高幅度激活，这些激活对模型优化至关重要。LongAct策略选择性地更新与这些显著激活相关的权重，从而实现了约8%的性能提升，并在RULER基准上增强了模型的泛化能力。我们的研究表明，关注这些显著特征是释放长上下文潜力的关键。

papers.title: DR^{3}-Eval: Towards Realistic and Reproducible Deep Research Evaluation
papers.abstract: Abstract DR$^{3}$-Eval is a benchmark for evaluating deep research agents on multimodal, multi-file report generation, featuring a realistic simulation of web environments and a comprehensive evaluation framework.  AI-generated summary Deep Research Agents (DRAs) aim to solve complex, long-horizon research tasks involving planning, retrieval, multimodal understanding, and report generation, yet their evaluation remains challenging due to dynamic web environments and ambiguous task definitions. We propose DR^{3}-Eval, a realistic and reproducible benchmark for evaluating deep research agents on multimodal, multi-file report generation. DR^{3}-Eval is constructed from authentic user-provided materials and paired with a per-task static research sandbox corpus that simulates open-web complexity while remaining fully verifiable, containing supportive documents, distractors, and noise. Moreover, we introduce a multi-dimensional evaluation framework measuring Information Recall, Factual Accuracy, Citation Coverage, Instruction Following, and Depth Quality, and validate its alignment with human judgments. Experiments with our developed multi-agent system DR^{3}-Agent based on multiple state-of-the-art language models demonstrate that DR^{3}-Eval is highly challenging and reveals critical failure modes in retrieval robustness and hallucination control. Our code and data are publicly available.
affiliations: Nanjing University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Бенчмарк для оценки агентов исследования с мультимодальной генерацией отчётов
ru.desc: В работе представлен DR³-Eval — бенчмарк для оценки агентов глубокого исследования, которые выполняют сложные многомодальные задачи с использованием планирования, поиска информации и генерации отчётов. Авторы создали реалистичную воспроизводимую среду, имитирующую веб-экосистему с подтверждающими документами, отвлекающей информацией и шумом. Предложена многомерная система оценивания, включающая измерение полноты информации, фактической точности, корректности цитирования и качества глубины анализа. Экспериментальные результаты показали, что существующие модели сталкиваются с критическими проблемами в робастности поиска и контроле галлюцинаций.

en.title: Evaluating AI Research Agents with DR$^{3}$-Eval
en.desc: The paper introduces DR$^{3}$-Eval, a benchmark designed to assess deep research agents (DRAs) in generating reports from multiple files and modalities. It addresses the complexities of evaluating AI systems in dynamic web environments, where tasks can be ambiguous and challenging. The benchmark is built using real user materials and includes a static research sandbox to simulate the open web, ensuring verifiability and relevance. Additionally, it features a comprehensive evaluation framework that measures various aspects of performance, such as information recall and factual accuracy, and highlights the limitations of current models in handling retrieval and hallucination issues.

zh.title: 深度研究代理的评估新标准
zh.desc: DR$^{3}$-Eval是一个用于评估深度研究代理在多模态、多文件报告生成方面的基准测试。该基准模拟了真实的网络环境，并提供了全面的评估框架，以应对复杂的研究任务。我们提出的评估框架包括信息召回、事实准确性、引用覆盖、指令遵循和深度质量等多个维度。实验结果表明，DR$^{3}$-Eval具有很高的挑战性，并揭示了在检索鲁棒性和幻觉控制方面的关键失败模式。

papers.title: C2: Scalable Rubric-Augmented Reward Modeling from Binary Preferences
papers.abstract: Abstract Cooperative yet Critical reward modeling (C2) enhances reward model reliability by enabling critical collaboration between a reward model and a rubric generator trained exclusively from binary preferences, achieving superior performance without requiring costly rubric annotations.  AI-generated summary Rubric-augmented verification guides reward models with explicit evaluation criteria, yielding more reliable judgments than single-model verification. However, most existing methods require costly rubric annotations, limiting scalability. Moreover, we find that rubric generation is vulnerable to a failure of cooperation; low-quality rubrics actively mislead reward models rather than help. Inspired by the principle of cooperative communication, we propose Cooperative yet Critical reward modeling (C2), a framework that significantly improves reward model judgments by having the reward model critically collaborate with a rubric generator trained solely from binary preferences. In C2, we synthesize helpful and misleading rubric pairs by measuring how each rubric shifts the reward model toward or away from the correct preference. Using these contrastive pairs, we train a cooperative rubric generator to propose helpful rubrics, and a critical verifier to assess rubric validity before making its judgment, following only rubrics it deems helpful at inference time. C2 outperforms reasoning reward models trained on the same binary preferences, with gains of up to 6.5 points on RM-Bench and 6.0 points length-controlled win rate on AlpacaEval 2.0. Without external rubric annotations, C2 enables an 8B reward model to match performance achieved with rubrics from a 4times larger model. Overall, our work demonstrates that eliciting deliberate cooperation in rubric-augmented verification makes reward models more trustworthy in a scalable way.
affiliations: The Graduate University for Advanced Studies (SOKENDAI)

ru.title: Критическое сотрудничество для надёжной оценки качества ответов
ru.desc: В работе предлагается метод C2 для улучшения надёжности моделей награды путём критического сотрудничества между моделью награды и генератором критериев оценки, обучаемым только на бинарных предпочтениях. Ключевая идея заключается в создании пар полезных и вводящих в заблуждение рубрик, которые измеряют, как каждая рубрика влияет на предпочтения модели награды. Модель обучается генерировать полезные критерии и проверять их валидность перед использованием, отклоняя те рубрики, которые сочтены неэффективными. Результаты показывают, что C2 превосходит другие методы обучения моделей награды на тех же бинарных предпочтениях, позволяя небольшой модели достичь производительности значительно более крупных моделей без дорогостоящих аннотаций.

en.title: Enhancing Reward Model Reliability through Cooperative Collaboration
en.desc: The paper introduces Cooperative yet Critical reward modeling (C2), a framework that enhances the reliability of reward models by fostering a critical collaboration between a reward model and a rubric generator. This approach utilizes rubrics generated from binary preferences, avoiding the need for expensive rubric annotations while still achieving high performance. C2 improves the quality of reward model judgments by training a cooperative rubric generator to create helpful rubrics and a critical verifier to assess their validity. The results show that C2 significantly outperforms traditional methods, demonstrating a scalable solution for more trustworthy reward modeling.

zh.title: 合作与关键：提升奖励模型的可靠性
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为合作但关键的奖励建模（C2）的方法，旨在提高奖励模型的可靠性。C2通过让奖励模型与仅基于二元偏好的评分生成器进行关键合作，避免了昂贵的评分注释需求。该方法通过合成有用和误导性的评分对，训练合作评分生成器和关键验证器，从而提升了奖励模型的判断能力。实验结果表明，C2在多个基准测试中表现优于传统的奖励模型，证明了这种合作方式在可扩展性和可靠性上的优势。

papers.title: Dive into Claude Code: The Design Space of Today's and Future AI Agent Systems
papers.abstract: Abstract The study analyzes Claude Code's architecture, identifying five motivating human values and tracing them through thirteen design principles to specific implementation choices, including a core while-loop architecture and supporting systems for safety, context management, and extensibility.  AI-generated summary Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that can run shell commands, edit files, and call external services on behalf of the user. This study describes its comprehensive architecture by analyzing the publicly available TypeScript source code and further comparing it with OpenClaw, an independent open-source AI agent system that answers many of the same design questions from a different deployment context. Our analysis identifies five human values, philosophies, and needs that motivate the architecture (human decision authority, safety and security, reliable execution, capability amplification, and contextual adaptability) and traces them through thirteen design principles to specific implementation choices. The core of the system is a simple while-loop that calls the model, runs tools, and repeats. Most of the code, however, lives in the systems around this loop: a permission system with seven modes and an ML-based classifier, a five-layer compaction pipeline for context management, four extensibility mechanisms (MCP, plugins, skills, and hooks), a subagent delegation mechanism with worktree isolation, and append-oriented session storage. A comparison with OpenClaw, a multi-channel personal assistant gateway, shows that the same recurring design questions produce different architectural answers when the deployment context changes: from per-action safety classification to perimeter-level access control, from a single CLI loop to an embedded runtime within a gateway control plane, and from context-window extensions to gateway-wide capability registration. We finally identify six open design directions for future agent systems, grounded in recent empirical, architectural, and policy literature.
affiliations: VILA Lab, Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence

ru.title: От человеческих ценностей к дизайну агентных систем: архитектура Claude Code
ru.desc: В работе анализируется архитектура Claude Code — агентной системы для автоматизации кодирования, которая выполняет команды, редактирует файлы и взаимодействует с внешними сервисами. Авторы выявили пять ключевых человеческих ценностей (контроль пользователя, безопасность, надежность, расширение возможностей и адаптивность), которые реализуются через тринадцать дизайн-принципов и конкретные технические решения. Ядро системы представляет собой простой цикл, вызывающий LLM и выполняющий инструменты, а основная часть кода сосредоточена в системах контроля доступа, управления контекстом и расширяемости. Сравнение с OpenClaw демонстрирует, что одинаковые архитектурные вопросы получают разные решения в зависимости от контекста развертывания системы.

en.title: Designing AI with Human Values in Mind
en.desc: This paper explores the architecture of Claude Code, an AI coding tool that executes commands and manages files for users. It identifies five key human values that influence its design, such as safety and contextual adaptability, and connects these values to thirteen design principles that guide its implementation. The core functionality is based on a while-loop structure that integrates various systems for permissions, context management, and extensibility. Additionally, the study compares Claude Code with OpenClaw, highlighting how different deployment contexts lead to distinct architectural solutions for similar design challenges.

zh.title: 探索Claude Code架构中的人类价值观
zh.desc: 本研究分析了Claude Code的架构，识别出五个人类价值观，并通过十三个设计原则追踪到具体的实现选择。这些价值观包括人类决策权、安全性、可靠执行、能力增强和上下文适应性。系统的核心是一个简单的while循环，负责调用模型、运行工具并重复执行。通过与OpenClaw的比较，研究表明不同的部署环境会导致相同设计问题产生不同的架构解决方案。

papers.title: Cross-Tokenizer LLM Distillation through a Byte-Level Interface
papers.abstract: Abstract Byte-Level Distillation enables cross-tokenizer knowledge transfer by operating at the byte level, achieving competitive performance compared to complex existing methods.  AI-generated summary Cross-tokenizer distillation (CTD), the transfer of knowledge from a teacher to a student language model when the two use different tokenizers, remains a largely unsolved problem. Existing approaches rely on heuristic strategies to align mismatched vocabularies, introducing considerable complexity. In this paper, we propose a simple but effective baseline called Byte-Level Distillation (BLD) which enables CTD by operating at a common interface across tokenizers: the byte level. In more detail, we convert the teacher's output distribution to byte-level probabilities, attach a lightweight byte-level decoder head to the student, and distill through this shared byte-level interface. Despite its simplicity, BLD performs competitively with--and on several benchmarks surpasses--significantly more sophisticated CTD methods, across a range of distillation tasks with models from 1B to 8B parameters. Our results suggest that the byte level is a natural common ground for cross-tokenizer knowledge transfer, while also highlighting that consistent improvements across all tasks and benchmarks remain elusive, underscoring that CTD is still an open problem.

ru.title: Байт-уровневая дистилляция как универсальный мост между токенизаторами
ru.desc: Работа предлагает метод дистилляции знаний между языковыми моделями с разными токенизаторами на уровне байтов. Вместо сложных эвристических подходов для выравнивания словарей, авторы используют общий байт-уровневый интерфейс как универсальное представление. Метод включает преобразование распределения вероятностей учителя в байт-уровневые вероятности и добавление лёгкого декодера студенту для дистилляции через общее представление. Несмотря на простоту, предложенный подход показывает конкурентные или лучшие результаты по сравнению с существующими методами на моделях от 1B до 8B параметров.

en.title: Simplifying Knowledge Transfer with Byte-Level Distillation
en.desc: This paper introduces Byte-Level Distillation (BLD), a method for transferring knowledge between language models that use different tokenizers. By operating at the byte level, BLD simplifies the process of cross-tokenizer distillation (CTD) and avoids the complexity of aligning different vocabularies. The authors demonstrate that BLD achieves competitive performance compared to more complex existing methods, even outperforming them on several benchmarks. This approach highlights the byte level as an effective common interface for knowledge transfer, while also acknowledging that challenges in CTD remain.

zh.title: 字节级蒸馏：简化跨分词器知识转移的有效方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为字节级蒸馏（Byte-Level Distillation, BLD）的方法，旨在解决跨分词器知识转移的问题。通过在字节级别进行操作，BLD能够简化不同分词器之间的知识传递，避免了复杂的对齐策略。实验结果表明，尽管方法简单，BLD在多个基准测试中表现出色，甚至超过了一些复杂的现有方法。这表明字节级别是跨分词器知识转移的自然共同基础，但在所有任务和基准上仍需进一步改进。

papers.title: LeapAlign: Post-Training Flow Matching Models at Any Generation Step by Building Two-Step Trajectories
papers.abstract: Abstract LeapAlign improves flow matching model fine-tuning by reducing computational costs and enabling stable gradient propagation through shortened trajectory steps while maintaining alignment with human preferences.  AI-generated summary This paper focuses on the alignment of flow matching models with human preferences. A promising way is fine-tuning by directly backpropagating reward gradients through the differentiable generation process of flow matching. However, backpropagating through long trajectories results in prohibitive memory costs and gradient explosion. Therefore, direct-gradient methods struggle to update early generation steps, which are crucial for determining the global structure of the final image. To address this issue, we introduce LeapAlign, a fine-tuning method that reduces computational cost and enables direct gradient propagation from reward to early generation steps. Specifically, we shorten the long trajectory into only two steps by designing two consecutive leaps, each skipping multiple ODE sampling steps and predicting future latents in a single step. By randomizing the start and end timesteps of the leaps, LeapAlign leads to efficient and stable model updates at any generation step. To better use such shortened trajectories, we assign higher training weights to those that are more consistent with the long generation path. To further enhance gradient stability, we reduce the weights of gradient terms with large magnitude, instead of completely removing them as done in previous works. When fine-tuning the Flux model, LeapAlign consistently outperforms state-of-the-art GRPO-based and direct-gradient methods across various metrics, achieving superior image quality and image-text alignment.

ru.title: Эффективное выравнивание генеративных моделей через укороченные траектории
ru.desc: LeapAlign — это метод для настройки моделей flow matching путём выравнивания с предпочтениями людей. Основная проблема заключается в том, что обратное распространение градиентов награды через длинные траектории генерации требует огромных вычислительных ресурсов и приводит к взрыву градиентов. Авторы предложили сокращать траекторию до двух этапов с помощью специальных скачков, пропускающих несколько шагов ODE-решателя, что позволяет эффективно обновлять ранние этапы генерации. Дополнительно используется взвешивание траекторий по их согласованности с полной генерацией и стабилизация градиентов, что даёт лучшее качество изображений и выравнивание с текстом.

en.title: LeapAlign: Efficient Fine-Tuning for Flow Matching Models
en.desc: LeapAlign is a novel fine-tuning method designed to enhance flow matching models by improving computational efficiency and ensuring stable gradient propagation. It addresses the challenges of backpropagating through long trajectories, which can lead to high memory usage and gradient explosion. By shortening the trajectory to just two steps and utilizing two consecutive leaps, LeapAlign allows for effective updates to early generation steps that are critical for the overall image structure. The method also incorporates a strategy to assign training weights based on alignment with longer generation paths, resulting in improved image quality and alignment with text descriptions.

zh.title: LeapAlign：高效稳定的流匹配模型微调方法
zh.desc: LeapAlign是一种改进流匹配模型微调的方法，通过缩短轨迹步骤来降低计算成本，并实现稳定的梯度传播，同时保持与人类偏好的对齐。该方法通过直接反向传播奖励梯度，解决了长轨迹反向传播带来的内存成本和梯度爆炸问题。LeapAlign通过设计两个连续的跳跃步骤，将长轨迹缩短为仅两个步骤，从而提高了模型更新的效率和稳定性。在微调Flux模型时，LeapAlign在多个指标上始终优于最先进的GRPO和直接梯度方法，获得了更好的图像质量和图像-文本对齐效果。

papers.title: MM-WebAgent: A Hierarchical Multimodal Web Agent for Webpage Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract MM-WebAgent is a hierarchical agentic framework that coordinates AIGC-based element generation for coherent and visually consistent webpage design through joint optimization of layout and multimodal content.  AI-generated summary The rapid progress of Artificial Intelligence Generated Content (AIGC) tools enables images, videos, and visualizations to be created on demand for webpage design, offering a flexible and increasingly adopted paradigm for modern UI/UX. However, directly integrating such tools into automated webpage generation often leads to style inconsistency and poor global coherence, as elements are generated in isolation. We propose MM-WebAgent, a hierarchical agentic framework for multimodal webpage generation that coordinates AIGC-based element generation through hierarchical planning and iterative self-reflection. MM-WebAgent jointly optimizes global layout, local multimodal content, and their integration, producing coherent and visually consistent webpages. We further introduce a benchmark for multimodal webpage generation and a multi-level evaluation protocol for systematic assessment. Experiments demonstrate that MM-WebAgent outperforms code-generation and agent-based baselines, especially on multimodal element generation and integration. Code & Data: https://aka.ms/mm-webagent.

ru.title: Согласованная генерация веб-страниц через иерархическую координацию мультимодального контента
ru.desc: MM-WebAgent — это иерархическая агентская система для автоматического создания веб-страниц с использованием инструментов генеративного ИИ. Фреймворк решает проблему несогласованности стилей при независимой генерации элементов, координируя создание контента на нескольких уровнях абстракции. Система одновременно оптимизирует глобальный макет страницы, локальное мультимодальное содержимое и их интеграцию через иерархическое планирование и итеративную саморефлексию. Авторы предложили бенчмарк и многоуровневый протокол оценки, показав превосходство подхода над методами на основе кодогенерации.

en.title: Coherent Webpages through Hierarchical AIGC Coordination
en.desc: The paper introduces MM-WebAgent, a framework designed to improve the generation of webpages using Artificial Intelligence Generated Content (AIGC). It addresses the common issue of style inconsistency and lack of coherence when AIGC tools create webpage elements in isolation. By employing hierarchical planning and iterative self-reflection, MM-WebAgent optimizes both the layout and the integration of multimodal content. The framework not only enhances the visual consistency of webpages but also sets a new benchmark for evaluating multimodal webpage generation.

zh.title: 智能网页设计的新纪元：MM-WebAgent
zh.desc: MM-WebAgent是一个层次化的智能框架，旨在通过联合优化网页布局和多模态内容，协调基于人工智能生成内容（AIGC）的元素生成，以实现一致且视觉上协调的网页设计。随着AIGC工具的快速发展，网页设计中可以按需生成图像、视频和可视化内容，但直接将这些工具集成到自动化网页生成中，常常导致风格不一致和整体连贯性差。MM-WebAgent通过层次规划和迭代自我反思，优化全球布局和局部多模态内容及其整合，生成一致且视觉上协调的网页。实验结果表明，MM-WebAgent在多模态元素生成和整合方面优于代码生成和基于智能体的基线。

papers.title: KV Packet: Recomputation-Free Context-Independent KV Caching for LLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract KV Packet is a cache reuse framework that eliminates recomputation overhead in large language models by treating cached documents as immutable packets with trainable soft-token adapters.  AI-generated summary Large Language Models (LLMs) rely heavily on Key-Value (KV) caching to minimize inference latency. However, standard KV caches are context-dependent: reusing a cached document in a new context requires recomputing KV states to account for shifts in attention distribution. Existing solutions such as CacheBlend, EPIC, and SAM-KV mitigate this issue by selectively recomputing a subset of tokens; however, they still incur non-negligible computational overhead (FLOPs) and increased Time-to-First-Token (TTFT) latency. In this paper, we propose KV Packet, a recomputation-free cache reuse framework that treats cached documents as immutable ``packets'' wrapped in light-weight trainable soft-token adapters, which are trained via self-supervised distillation to bridge context discontinuities. Experiments on Llama-3.1 and Qwen2.5 demonstrate that the proposed KV Packet method achieves near-zero FLOPs and lower TTFT than recomputation-based baselines, while retaining F1 scores comparable to those of the full recomputation baseline.

ru.title: Кэширование без пересчёта: адаптеры вместо переобработки
ru.desc: KV Packet — это фреймворк для повторного использования кэша в больших языковых моделях, который избегает пересчёта KV-состояний при изменении контекста. Вместо пересчёта токенов предлагается рассматривать закэшированные документы как неизменяемые пакеты, обёрнутые в обучаемые адаптеры мягких токенов. Эти адаптеры обучаются методом самостоятельной дистилляции для преодоления разрывов в контексте. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что метод достигает минимальных вычислительных затрат и низкой задержки первого токена, сохраняя качество вывода на уровне полного пересчёта.

en.title: Revolutionizing Cache Reuse with KV Packet
en.desc: The paper introduces KV Packet, a novel framework designed to enhance cache reuse in large language models (LLMs) by eliminating the need for recomputation. It treats cached documents as immutable packets, utilizing lightweight trainable soft-token adapters to manage context shifts without recalculating key-value states. This approach significantly reduces computational overhead and latency during inference, achieving near-zero FLOPs and faster Time-to-First-Token (TTFT) compared to existing methods. Experimental results show that KV Packet maintains performance levels similar to full recomputation while improving efficiency.

zh.title: KV Packet：消除大型语言模型的重新计算开销
zh.desc: KV Packet是一种缓存重用框架，旨在消除大型语言模型中的重新计算开销。它将缓存的文档视为不可变的“数据包”，并使用可训练的软令牌适配器来处理上下文变化。通过自监督蒸馏训练，这种方法能够有效地桥接上下文间的差异。实验结果表明，KV Packet在计算效率和延迟方面优于传统的重新计算方法，同时保持了与完整重新计算基线相当的F1分数。
